Checklist Item: Skip links implemented
Comments: No skip-to-main (or similar) link found among first focusable links; DOM query for anchor skip/main content patterns returned none.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse accessibility score >= 90 in Browser Audit Lighthouse panel; zero critical (wcag2a/wcag2aa) accessibility violations reported; all flagged items reviewed and addressed or documented
Comments: Lighthouse accessibility 95 (>=90) but failed audits remain: aria-allowed-attr (wcag2a/critical) and label-content-name-mismatch (wcag21a). Not zero critical WCAG violations.

Checklist Item: No inline scripts without nonce/hash (CSP compliant)
Comments: Multiple inline <script> blocks in document without nonce/hash attributes (including module, JSON-LD, GTM snippets).

Checklist Item: Form actions use HTTPS only
Comments: All forms use relative actions (/us/... or default POST to same HTTPS origin); no http:// action URLs.

Checklist Item: Lazy loading for below-the-fold images
Comments: 18/29 use loading="lazy"; eager used on header logos and hero imagery appropriate for LCP. Remaining non-lazy images appear above-the-fold or decorative.

Checklist Item: CSS delivery optimized
Comments: Many render-blocking stylesheet link tags (~14) including third-party Swiper CSS; no clear critical/non-critical split or async pattern observed in DOM.

Checklist Item: Unused JS removed
Comments: Heavy third-party JS (GTM, Adobe Launch, YouTube iframe API) loaded on landing page; Lighthouse run available did not expose unused-bytes audit—by inspection large tag-manager stacks remain.

Checklist Item: Meta description tag present on all pages; content between 50-160 characters; not a default placeholder value (e.g. 'description' / 'website' / 'page')
Comments: Meta description present but 167 characters (exceeds 50–160 guideline).

Checklist Item: Favicon (<link rel="icon">) present in <head>; Apple touch icon present where applicable
Comments: No <link rel="icon"> or apple-touch-icon in document head (only canonical and stylesheets); browser may still fetch /favicon.ico implicitly.

Checklist Item: Canonical URL (<link rel="canonical">) present on all indexable pages; href is absolute URL; no duplicate canonical tags per page
Comments: Single canonical link with absolute HTTPS URL matching page.

Checklist Item: Alternate language annotations (hreflang) present for multilingual sites; each hreflang is valid BCP 47 tag; x-default fallback present
Comments: No hreflang alternates in head; page targets single /us/ locale without alternate language URLs—hreflang set not required for this page type.

Checklist Item: CSS <link> and <style> tags appear before <script> tags in <head> section; no JavaScript-only <script> inserted before stylesheet <link>
Comments: Head ordering: first script node index precedes final stylesheet link (scripts interleaved with/after CSS). Requirement is all CSS before scripts.

Checklist Item: External links with target="_blank" include rel="noopener noreferrer" to prevent tab nabbing and referrer leakage
Comments: 12+ anchor tags with target=_blank lack rel containing both noopener and noreferrer (e.g. news cards, social footer links).

Checklist Item: Custom error pages for 404 and 5xx HTTP responses exist in source; error pages include site branding and navigation back to homepage
Comments: 404 path shows branded layout (header/footer), message, and "Go to Home" to /us/gaming-dt. Server 5xx error page not triggered in this audit.

Checklist Item: Webfont files use WOFF2 as primary format with WOFF fallback; @font-face declarations include format() hints matching served file types
Comments: @font-face rules use WOFF2 URLs with format("woff2") hints; no WOFF fallback src line present in fonts.css.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse performance score >= 90 on desktop and >= 90 on mobile; run via Browser Audit Lighthouse panel (Audits tab) with device and throttling set appropriately
Comments: Chrome DevTools Lighthouse MCP output omits Performance category score; cannot read desktop/mobile >=90 from Audits panel via automation.

Checklist Item: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 2.5s; CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1; INP (Interaction to Next Paint) < 200ms; verify via Browser Audit Performance panel recording or Lighthouse Core Web Vitals section
Comments: Performance trace (reload, no CPU/network throttle): LCP ~316ms and CLS 0.00 meet thresholds; INP not measured because no interaction sequence was recorded.

Checklist Item: No JavaScript long tasks (> 50ms) blocking the main thread during page load; Total Blocking Time (TBT) within budget; verify via Browser Audit Performance panel Main thread flame chart and Lighthouse TBT metric
Comments: Third-party insight: Google Tag Manager alone consumed ~118ms main-thread time during load—likely includes >50ms tasks; TBT/Lighthouse TBT not exported.

Checklist Item: Time to First Byte (TTFB) < 800ms measured in Browser Audit Network panel Timing tab; no excessive DNS lookup or TCP/TLS connection time in waterfall
Comments: Performance trace LCP breakdown shows TTFB ~1ms on lab reload (CDN); well under 800ms budget. DNS/TLS not excessive in waterfall snapshot.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse SEO score >= 90 and Best Practices score >= 90 in Browser Audit Lighthouse panel; no critical findings blocking either category
Comments: Lighthouse navigation (desktop): Best Practices 96 (passes) but SEO 61 (<90). robots noindex and other SEO audits likely contributing.

Checklist Item: Total page weight within performance budget (< 1MB transferred for initial load); verify via Browser Audit Network panel Summary row showing transferred size, resource count, and finish time
Comments: Third-party insight reported ~1.3MB Google Tag Manager transfer alone; total transferred bytes exceed 1MB initial-load budget despite same-origin Resource Timing under-reporting.

Checklist Item: Text resources (HTML/CSS/JS) served with gzip or Brotli compression; verify via Content-Encoding response header in Browser Audit Network panel Headers tab for each critical resource
Comments: HEAD checks on HTML (earlier), CSS, and JS show Content-Encoding: gzip for critical text resources on origin.

Checklist Item: All images within size budget; no uncompressed PNG served where WebP/AVIF is available; image display dimensions match intrinsic dimensions (no oversized images scaled via CSS); verify in Browser Audit Network panel and Lighthouse image audit
Comments: Network shows large PNG/WebP pairs for product imagery; TUF thumbnail still fetched as PNG alongside WebP for other SKUs—likely oversized vs display without Lighthouse image sizing proof.

Checklist Item: Resources served over HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 protocol; verify via Protocol column in Browser Audit Network panel (right-click column headers to enable Protocol column)
Comments: Resource Timing nextHopProtocol distribution: majority h2, some h3, no http/1.1 observed for measured resources.

Checklist Item: JavaScript and CSS coverage on critical pages shows < 50% unused bytes on initial load; verify via Browser Audit Coverage panel (Ctrl+Shift+P > Show Coverage); flag unused bytes for lazy loading or removal
Comments: Coverage panel data not available through Chrome DevTools MCP; unused byte percentage not measured.

Checklist Item: Content-Security-Policy (CSP) response header present with restrictive policy; no 'unsafe-inline' or 'unsafe-eval' directives without nonce/hash; verify in Browser Audit Network panel Response Headers for the main document request
Comments: Main document CSP includes script-src 'unsafe-inline' and style-src 'unsafe-inline' without nonces on inline blocks observed in HTML.

Checklist Item: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff present on all responses; X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors set to prevent clickjacking; Referrer-Policy header configured to restrict referrer leakage; verify all security headers in Browser Audit Network panel
Comments: Main document response headers include x-content-type-options:nosniff, x-frame-options:SAMEORIGIN, CSP frame-ancestors 'none', referrer-policy:strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

Checklist Item: Zero JavaScript errors or unhandled promise rejections in Browser Audit Console panel on page load and during core user interactions; no CORS policy errors; console filter set to Errors level during production audit
Comments: Console shows three errors from failed fetchHotProducts (HTTP 404) and downstream hot products load failure.

Checklist Item: No deprecated browser API usage warnings or browser compatibility warnings visible in Browser Audit Console panel on production URL; Verbose level checked for hidden debug output
Comments: Console verbose/issue scan shows no deprecated API warnings; only Issues tab notes (CSP eval restriction, unlabeled form control) unrelated to deprecated browser APIs.

Checklist Item: No mixed content warnings in Browser Audit Security panel; all sub-resources (scripts/styles/images/fonts/iframes) loaded over HTTPS; Security panel reports 'This page is secure (valid HTTPS)'
Comments: No http:// subresources observed in Resource Timing; page served over HTTPS with HTTPS/CDN assets.

Checklist Item: TLS certificate valid and not expired; certificate chain complete and trusted by browser; verify in Browser Audit Security panel Certificate section; no certificate warnings on page load
Comments: Page loaded in Chrome without certificate interstitials; TLS chain accepted by browser (Security panel not directly readable via MCP).

Checklist Item: No sensitive data (auth tokens; PII; credentials; session secrets) visible in Browser Audit Application panel under Local Storage; Session Storage; IndexedDB; or Cookies; HttpOnly and Secure flags set on authentication cookies
Comments: SessionStorage exposes `cto_token_us` key (token-like client-side storage); cannot verify HttpOnly/Secure flags on cookies from JavaScript surface.

Checklist Item: Cookies used for session management have SameSite attribute set to Strict or Lax; Secure flag enforced on all auth cookies; no unencrypted sensitive values in cookie payloads; verify in Browser Audit Application > Storage > Cookies
Comments: Cookie SameSite/Secure attributes not inspectable via available DevTools MCP tools (Application > Cookies UI not exported).

Checklist Item: Page layout tested at standard breakpoints using Browser Audit Device Toolbar responsive mode: 320px; 375px; 768px; 1024px; 1440px; no horizontal scrollbar at any mobile width; no content overflow or clipping detected
Comments: Chrome MCP viewport could not be reduced below ~500px width; true 320/375 breakpoint checks blocked. At smallest achieved width, no horizontal overflow (scrollWidth<=clientWidth).

Checklist Item: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1 verified in Browser Audit Performance panel recording; no unexpected layout shifts after fonts load or lazy-loaded images render; individual shift scores inspected in Experience section of timeline
Comments: Performance trace (navigation reload) reported CLS 0.00; no layout-shift spikes observed in summary.

Checklist Item: No render-blocking resources identified in Browser Audit Lighthouse report or Performance panel waterfall; third-party scripts do not appear in critical rendering path; async/defer attributes confirmed in Network timeline
Comments: Performance insight 'RenderBlocking' flagged; multiple render-blocking stylesheets and third-party tags load early in waterfall.

Checklist Item: No console.log or developer debug output active on production build; Browser Audit Console panel shows zero developer messages at Verbose/Info level when auditing production URL
Comments: Console log message present: "Attempting to fetch from: ...gameList.json" indicating debug-style logging on stage.

Checklist Item: No 'Violation' messages (handler took Xms; forced reflow while executing) in Browser Audit Console panel during user interactions; violations indicate long-running event handlers or layout thrashing caused by interleaved DOM reads and writes
Comments: After scroll interaction, console contains no Chrome '[Violation]' forced reflow / long handler messages (only existing fetch log and errors).

Checklist Item: No forced synchronous layouts (layout thrashing) visible in Browser Audit Performance panel flame chart; purple 'Recalculate Style' or 'Layout' blocks triggered inside JS call stacks flagged; scrolling and animation frames remain within 16ms budget
Comments: Performance trace summary does not expose detailed main-thread flame classification to rule out forced synchronous layouts inside JS stacks.

Checklist Item: No excessive repaints on scroll or CSS transitions; verify via Browser Audit Rendering panel > Paint flashing checkbox; green flash areas should be minimal and not cover full-page regions on scroll
Comments: Rendering > Paint flashing not accessible via Chrome DevTools MCP; repaint behavior on scroll not instrumented.

Checklist Item: No detached DOM nodes accumulating after component mount/unmount cycles or SPA route changes; verify via Browser Audit Memory panel > Take heap snapshot > filter by 'Detached'; zero detached HTMLElement nodes expected after GC
Comments: Heap snapshot/detached DOM filtering not performed or parsed via MCP (would require manual Memory panel analysis).

Checklist Item: Preloaded resources (<link rel='preload'>) are consumed within the page load; no 'The resource was preloaded using link preload but not used within a few seconds' warnings in Browser Audit Console; unused preloads waste bandwidth and block the load event
Comments: No <link rel="preload"> elements in document; therefore no unused-preload console warnings observed.

Checklist Item: Web fonts load without FOIT (Flash of Invisible Text); Performance panel film strip shows text visible during font load phase; font-display swap or optional strategy confirmed by absence of blank text frames in Network waterfall timing
Comments: fonts.css @font-face rules include font-display: swap for Roboto and TTNormsPro, mitigating FOIT.

Checklist Item: FCP (First Contentful Paint) < 1.8s and Speed Index < 3.4s on mobile throttled network; verify via Browser Audit Lighthouse panel or Performance panel timing markers; FCP marker visible in Network waterfall as vertical line
Comments: Performance trace after mobile viewport + Slow 4G emulation still shows "Network throttling: none" and omits FCP/Speed Index metrics; cannot confirm <1.8s FCP and <3.4s Speed Index on throttled mobile.

Checklist Item: No duplicate network requests for the same resource during page load; verify via Browser Audit Network panel sorted by Name column; duplicate URL entries indicate missing request deduplication or redundant script imports
Comments: Network log shows paired requests for identical media IDs at multiple widths (e.g. media_1f1d85… loaded with width=750 and again with width=2000) during single page load—duplicate decoding of same creative.

Checklist Item: WebSocket connections (if used) use encrypted wss:// protocol only; no unencrypted ws:// connections visible in Browser Audit Network panel WS filter; sensitive data must not transit over unencrypted WebSocket channels
Comments: No WebSocket connections recorded in Network panel WS filter; therefore no ws:// usage.

Checklist Item: No resources loaded from origins not listed in Content-Security-Policy; CSP violations visible in Browser Audit Console as 'Refused to load' messages; each violation indicates a policy misconfiguration or an unauthorized resource
Comments: Console shows no "Refused to load" CSP violation messages; third-party requests align with declared script-src/connect-src allowlists.
